title: Walmart teams with Alphabet for AI-assisted shopping on Gemini

Investor confidence is high following a 30% rally and a recent $15.4 million acquisition of a Sam's Club property expected to stabilize cash flow. Analysts recommend holding PINE shares, citing a 7% yield and an average 1-year price target of $17.94.

title: New Walmart CEO is not afraid to take risks, analyst says
summary: John Furner, the new CEO of Walmart, is described by analyst Oliver Chen as a leader with a higher appetite for risk than his predecessor, a necessary trait in a technology-driven environment. Furner plans to spend his initial weeks visiting stores and facilities, engaging directly with employees to understand their challenges. This approach reinforces the company's commitment to innovation and improving operations, as it navigates challenges from competitors like Amazon in both grocery and pharmacy sectors.

title: Target Grapples With Identity Questions Under New CEO
summary: Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, acknowledged in his first town hall that the retailer has lost trust with shoppers and employees, pledging to rebuild this connection and clarify the company's identity. The company faces challenges including a sales slump, an abrupt pullback from diversity initiatives, and pressure regarding its stance on federal immigration crackdowns. Fiddelke committed to improving merchandise, in-store experiences, and technology, while carefully navigating the immigration issue to prioritize employee safety.

title: Target’s new CEO admits retailer has lost trust with shoppers, staff: report
summary: Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, has acknowledged that the retailer has lost the trust of both shoppers and employees. He stated that the company was not clear enough about its identity, causing confusion that was not adequately addressed. Fiddelke committed to rebuilding these connections and is taking over at a time when Target faces a lengthy sales slump and has pulled back from diversity initiatives.

title: CFO Mandy Fields sees e.l.f. Beauty in growth mode, as company beats on sales and earnings
summary: e.l.f. Beauty reported strong fiscal third-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street expectations for both revenue and adjusted earnings per share. The company raised its full-year guidance, driven by accelerated growth from the rhode acquisition and robust performance in international markets. CFO Mandy Fields emphasized the company's focus on cosmetic and skin care segments, strong marketing ROI, and maintained value proposition across different retailers, while also noting slight headwinds in the UK and Germany.

title: E.l.f. Beauty Raises 2026 Forecasts as Rhode Breaks Records and New Super Bowl Campaign Debuts
summary: E.l.f. Beauty has increased its full-year 2026 forecasts for both revenue and net income following a strong third quarter. The company, which owns E.l.f., Rhode, and Naturium, reported a 38% surge in Q3 net sales to $489.5 million, surpassing analyst expectations. This positive outlook is driven by the continued international expansion and record-breaking performance of Rhode, along with Naturium's growth and E.l.f.'s upcoming Super Bowl campaign and new fragrance launch.

title: Trump shares video falsely claiming Walmart shuttering California stores
summary: Donald Trump shared an AI-generated video falsely claiming Walmart was closing over 250 stores in California due to a nonexistent $22 minimum wage implemented by Governor Gavin Newsom. Both Walmart and Newsom's office confirmed the claims are untrue, stating that Walmart has 303 stores in California and the state's minimum wage for general employers is $16.90, not $22. The video is part of a trend of AI-generated content spreading misinformation, with a spokesperson for Newsom noting Trump "does not know the difference between AI and reality."

title: Costco fans reportedly name dairy item as a must-buy that justifies membership
summary: Costco members are raving about Tillamook block cheese as a "must-buy" item that justifies the annual membership fee, attributing its value to significantly lower prices compared to other retailers. A 2.5-pound block costs around $11.23, or 28 cents an ounce, which is considerably cheaper than similar products at Walmart, Kroger, and Target. Shoppers have celebrated these savings, with one reported to have stocked up on 34 pounds of the cheese during a sale.

title: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S Lowers Stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST
summary: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S reduced its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation by 13.3%, selling 16,194 shares, now holding 105,956 shares valued at $98.08 million. Despite insider selling, Costco demonstrates strong operational momentum with raised January retail sales, significant membership revenue growth, and an announced quarterly dividend of $1.30. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $998.50, though some debate its high P/E valuation.

title: SBI Securities Co. Ltd. Sells 2,828 Shares of Invesco QQQ $QQQ
summary: SBI Securities Co. Ltd. reduced its stake in Invesco QQQ by 1.4% in the third quarter, selling 2,828 shares but still holding 194,954 shares valued at $117 million, making it its sixth largest holding. The Invesco QQQ recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.7941 per share, representing an annualized yield of approximately 0.5%. Institutional activity for QQQ shows a mixed picture, with some firms boosting holdings while others adjusted, and recent Nasdaq weakness and technical warnings suggest potential for further outflows.

title: 1 Bold Decision Helped Make Walmart a Trillion-Dollar Company. Most CEOs Do the Opposite
summary: Walmart recently joined the exclusive group of companies valued at over $1 trillion, a significant achievement attributed largely to former CEO Doug McMillon. While many experts cite technology and AI as the main drivers, McMillon's crucial decision to visit stores, listen to employees, and increase their pay was a foundational step that enabled these technological advancements. This bold move, which most CEOs would avoid, played a critical role in turning the company around from a challenging position when McMillon became CEO in 2014.

title: Kroger Shifts From Georgia Clinics To Marketplace Expansion And Investor Focus
summary: Kroger (NYSE:KR) is closing all of its Georgia-based Little Clinic health centers while simultaneously expanding its large-format Marketplace stores in several states. This strategic shift indicates Kroger's prioritization of higher-traffic retail formats and a focus on balancing growth investment with shareholder returns, including a consistent dividend. Investors will be watching how these changes impact store traffic, profitability, and the overall balance between health services and retail expansion.

title: The 5 Biggest Changes You'll Notice At Kroger In 2026
summary: Kroger is poised for significant changes in 2026, including a new partnership with Uber for faster grocery delivery, an expanded Simple Truth brand focusing on high-protein products, and the integration of AI-powered personal shopping assistants. The company will also introduce a Verified Savings Program for discounts to those in need and plans to open more Kroger Marketplace stores after strategic closures in 2025. These initiatives aim to enhance customer experience, promote healthier eating, and expand Kroger's market presence following its failed merger with Albertsons.

title: Avery Dennison Corporation (NYSE:AVY) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Avery Dennison Corporation announced solid full-year 2025 results with adjusted EPS of $9.53 and $700 million in adjusted free cash flow, exceeding earnings expectations with a reported EPS of $2.45. Despite organic sales being flat due to deflationary pricing and softer consumer sentiment, the company emphasized its focus on high-value categories, innovation acceleration, and productivity to drive future growth. For 2026, Avery Dennison anticipates approximately 6% adjusted earnings per share growth on organic sales growth of zero to 2%, with a sequential increase in earnings throughout the year.

title: Aterian Recalls PurSteam Elite Travel Steamers and Mighty Lil Steamers Due to Risk of Serious Burn Injury
summary: Aterian has recalled approximately 75,400 PurSteam Elite Travel Steamers and 119,000 PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamers due to a serious burn hazard. The steamers can expel hot water from the nozzle during use, with 80 incidents and 14 burn injuries reported for the Elite Travel Steamer, and 392 incidents with 40 burn injuries (including two second-degree) for the Mighty Lil Steamer. Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled products, cut the cord, and submit a refund request online.

title: Walmart introduces new minimum $40.50 rule across all US stores
summary: Walmart has announced a significant pay increase for its pharmacy team, particularly for newly created team leader and technician positions. Pharmacy team leaders will see their average pay rise from $28 to $42 per hour, and pharmacy technicians will go from $22 to $40.50 per hour, representing an 86% pay raise for technicians. This move is part of Walmart's investment in its Health & Wellness division and aims to strengthen the care experience in communities while providing clear career pathways for associates.

title: Impinj set to report earnings as retail mandates fuel RFID push
summary: Impinj Inc. is preparing to report its fourth-quarter earnings, with investors focused on whether the company can maintain its growth despite expected sequential revenue softness. The company is benefiting from expanded retail RFID mandates, such as Walmart's, and the integration of its technology by Avery Dennison. Key metrics to watch include gross margins and guidance related to the new Gen2X protocol enhancements, which are expected to boost inventory efficiency and combat counterfeiting.

title: Mizuho Raises Costco (COST) Target as Membership Trends Rebound

title: Mizuho Raises Costco (COST) Target as Membership Trends Rebound

While Costco's overall business remains strong with high sales and renewal rates, investors are watching how the company addresses these claims, as the outcome could influence consumer perception and potentially lead to financial and reputational risks.

This deal is among the largest retail transactions in Manhattan this year, following other substantial leases at Herald Towers.

title: Kroger to close Georgia health clinics

The Little Clinic will continue to operate in other states, and Kroger encourages patients to continue using its in-store pharmacies.

summary: Kroger is scaling back its in-store medical clinics, The Little Clinic, by closing all 18 locations in Georgia, following a trend seen with other major retailers like Walmart, Walgreens, and CVS. This decision comes despite strong growth in Kroger's pharmacy division, as retailers face challenges with high operating costs, a difficult reimbursement environment, and low margins in primary care services. The outcome could impact Costco's marketing, formulation costs, and how it communicates with members regarding its private label products, while investors will watch for potential legal exposure and brand reputation effects.

title: 2 arrested for string of thefts at Walmart stores in Orleans, Genesee, Monroe counties
summary: Two individuals, Kirk Thomas and Nevada Nichols, both from Batavia, have been arrested in connection with a series of thefts from Walmart stores in Orleans, Genesee, and Monroe counties, west of Rochester. The alleged thefts, primarily involving electronics, exceeded $1,400 from the Albion Walmart alone, with thousands more stolen from Batavia and Brockport locations. Thomas, who has an extensive criminal history with 48 previous arrests and eight felony convictions, was held in custody, while Nichols was released on appearance tickets.

title: Scalding Water Burns Dozens, Sparking Massive Clothing Steamer Recall
summary: Aterian, Inc. is recalling nearly 195,000 PurSteam-branded clothing steamers, including Mighty Lil Steamers and Elite Travel Steamers, due to a burn hazard. Consumers are advised to stop using the product, cut the cord, and submit a recall request online for a full refund.

title: Natural Grocers to open first Wisconsin store

The company met strict criteria for Return on Invested Capital (ROIC), debt management, profit quality, and growth steadiness. While Walmart's valuation is noted as high, its strong operational model and financial durability align with the principles of quality investing for long-term growth.

title: FSTA Offers Lower Fees While RSPS Pays Higher Dividends
summary: This article compares two consumer staples ETFs, FSTA and RSPS, highlighting their differences in expense ratios, dividend yields, and portfolio construction. FSTA offers lower fees and concentrates on sector giants, leading to better long-term performance, while RSPS has a higher dividend yield and uses an equal-weight approach, which may appeal to investors seeking greater stability. Investors must weigh cost against portfolio diversification and performance to decide which ETF best suits their defensive investment needs.

title: Costco stock price climbs into holiday break as inflation cools, lawsuit adds an edge
summary: Costco's stock price increased by 1.9% on Friday, reaching $1,018.48, driven by cooling U.S. inflation data which boosted consumer stocks. The company is set to release its fiscal second-quarter results and February sales update on March 5, which investors are keenly awaiting. However, a new class-action lawsuit alleging salmonella contamination risk in its rotisserie chicken supply chain introduces potential headline risk for the warehouse retailer.

title: Walmart (WMT) Is a Great American Company, Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer considers Walmart (WMT) a great American company, noting its strong stock performance with a 24% increase over the past year. Analysts like Oppenheimer, UBS, and Tigress Financial have raised price targets for Walmart, citing its successful integration of AI and automation. Cramer emphasizes Walmart's commitment to keeping prices low for American consumers, viewing its current success as a return to traditional investing focused on established, household-name companies.

title: Costco "Hot Fuel" Settlement Approved
summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. has agreed to a settlement in a "hot fuel" lawsuit, requiring them to convert gasoline pumps in certain states over the next five years to automatically compensate for fuel expansion due to temperature changes. While Costco will not pay a monetary settlement, plaintiffs' lawyers have requested $10 million in attorney fees. Other major companies like BP, ConocoPhillips, and Shell have also reached similar settlement agreements.

title: 3 High-Yield Dividend ETFs Perfect for Those Planning on Retiring
summary: This article identifies three high-yield dividend ETFs that are suitable for investors aged 60 and above who are seeking steady passive income and diversification. The Vanguard High Dividend Yield ETF (VYM), Schwab International Dividend Equity ETF (SCHY), and Fidelity High Dividend ETF (FDVV) are highlighted for their dividend yields, diversification, and reasonable expense ratios. These ETFs offer a way for retirees to generate income with mitigated risk and potential for capital appreciation.

title: Walmart To Lead Group Of 11 Companies Announcing Annual Dividend Increases In Second Half Of February
summary: Walmart Inc. is anticipated to declare its 53rd consecutive yearly dividend increase in late February, with projections ranging from 6.4% to 9.6%. While Walmart experienced accelerated dividend growth in recent years, a slowdown in EPS growth to approximately 4% for FY26 suggests a moderation to high single-digit dividend increases. Other companies expected to announce similar dividend hikes include Eaton and Steel Dynamics with notable double-digit increases, and Dividend Kings Genuine Parts and Coca-Cola continuing their multi-decade growth streaks.

title: Barclays: Core Shoppers Shift to Walmart and Dollar Stores in 2026
summary: A Barclays analysis indicates that "core" conventional grocery shoppers are increasingly prioritizing price in 2026, leading to a significant acceleration in Walmart's unit share. This shift is also benefiting dollar stores, while specialty retailers and large public grocers are struggling. The trend highlights a broader consumer migration towards value-oriented retailers amid persistent economic pressures.

title: Grocery giant 'better than Walmart' to open 8 stores across 7 states this summer
summary: Trader Joe's is set to expand its presence across the US with the opening of eight new stores in seven states by the end of summer. These new locations will be in Arizona, Florida, Georgia, Kansas, Washington, and two in Louisiana, in addition to previously announced stores in New Orleans, Louisiana, and McKinney, Texas. The company plans to continue looking for more new neighborhood store locations annually.

title: SKYX Announces Launch at Walmart, U.S. Leading Retailer, of its Ceiling Plug & Play SKYFAN & TURBO HEATER
summary: SKYX Platforms Corp. announced the launch of its patented ceiling plug & play SKYFAN & TURBO HEATER at Walmart, anticipating significant growth in 2026. This innovative product combines a ceiling fan with a built-in turbo heater, offering a safer and more efficient alternative to traditional space heaters. The company expects additional winter launches in other leading U.S. retailers and projects this launch will advance its path to cash-flow positive.

title: Instacart stock jumps on upbeat outlook; what could move CART next week
summary: Instacart (Maplebear Inc.) stock rose 9.2% on Friday to $36.30 after reporting strong quarterly results and an upbeat outlook, with first-quarter gross transaction value projected between $10.13 billion and $10.28 billion. The company's advertising revenue also surpassed $1 billion in 2025, signaling a promising core business performance. However, competition from DoorDash, Uber Eats, and Amazon, along with the challenge of maintaining double-digit grocery delivery growth, pose potential headwinds for CART as markets resume after the Presidents Day holiday.

title: Cadence Design Systems Earnings: What To Look For From CDNS
summary: Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS) is set to announce its earnings results, with analysts expecting a 5.1% year-on-year revenue growth to $1.43 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.91 per share. The company has a history of beating revenue estimates, and analysts have largely reconfirmed their estimates. Peers like Procore Technologies and Dolby Laboratories have shown mixed results, and the design software segment has generally underperformed in a choppy economic environment.

title: XLP vs. FTXG: The Clash of Consumer Staple ETFs
summary: This article compares two consumer staple ETFs, XLP and FTXG, highlighting their costs, returns, risks, and portfolio constructions. While FTXG focuses on food and beverage companies with a higher expense ratio and boasts a slightly higher dividend yield, XLP, with a longer track record and lower expense ratio, has significantly outperformed FTXG in both one-year and five-year returns. The analysis concludes that XLP generally offers better performance and stability, though FTXG may have scalability potential as a younger fund.

title: PPL Surpasses 3% Yield Threshold
summary: PPL Corporation is highlighted for surpassing a 3% yield threshold, with analysis suggesting a reasonable expectation for a 3% annual yield based on historical performance, despite dividend unpredictability. Wall Street analysts forecast a rise in PPL stock price, though one analyst, Barclays, recently downgraded its price target due to adjustments in the power and utilities group. Morgan Stanley, however, maintained an Overweight rating and raised its price target for PPL.

title: What To Expect From Genuine Parts's (GPC) Q4 Earnings
summary: Genuine Parts (GPC) is set to report its Q4 earnings before the bell on Tuesday. Analysts anticipate a 5% year-on-year revenue increase to $6.06 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.82 per share. The company has a mixed record on revenue estimates, and its stock has performed well recently, up 13.4% over the last month.

title: Dollar Tree Stock Is On Sale: Smart Money Move Or Total Trap Right Now?
summary: Dollar Tree's stock has recently fallen, leading to Wall Street uncertainty regarding whether it presents a buying opportunity or a value trap. It contrasts Dollar Tree with competitors like Dollar General, Walmart, and Target, offering a nuanced perspective on whether investors should consider buying or selling the stock based on their risk tolerance and investment goals.

title: Chipotle Mexican Grill planned for The Pavilion at Durbin Park

The restaurant will occupy a 2,800-square-foot space, formerly Rumble Boxing, and will not include a drive-thru. This development is part of a broader expansion in St. Johns County, with other locations already open or planned.

title: Lower prices? Higher wages? What choice of new Kroger CEO foretells
summary: Kroger's new CEO, Greg Foran, is expected to bring significant changes to the company, potentially focusing on lower prices, store overhauls, and e-commerce enhancements. Analysts predict these moves based on his previous tenure at Walmart's U.S. stores, where he also prioritized boosting employee pay and training. The article examines what these strategic choices could mean for the Cincinnati-based supermarket giant.

title: ABN Amro Investment Solutions Has $5.76 Million Holdings in Comcast Corporation $CMCSA
summary: ABN Amro Investment Solutions significantly reduced its stake in Comcast Corporation by 50.9% in Q3, selling 190,189 shares and retaining 183,154 shares valued at $5.76 million. Cavanagh, also sold a portion of his shares, while analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus rating with a $34.90 target price.

summary: Occidental Petroleum is set to report its Q4 and full-year earnings on February 18. title: Markets Brief: The AI Loser Trade, and What’s Driving It
summary: This article discusses the "AI Loser Trade," where investors are selling stocks of companies whose business models are perceived to be vulnerable to disruption from new artificial intelligence technologies. The article also touches on the shift towards equal-weighted indexes and anticipates upcoming earnings reports from major retailers and the release of Q4 2025 GDP data.

This technology allows for dynamic content, such as product videos, seasonal promotions, and educational messaging, aiming to enhance the shopping experience and drive sales. CVS has already deployed screens in over 600 locations, while Kroger is prioritizing grocery aisles and health/beauty sections, viewing end caps as a crucial point of influence for customer engagement.

(NYSE:FND) by selling 21,202 shares, decreasing its total holdings to 487,440 shares valued at approximately $35.9 million. The report suggests Walmart is solidifying its dominance against competitors like Amazon, Target, and Costco and is well-positioned for future growth.

title: These Popular Grocery Stores Are All Part Of The Kroger Family

It highlights Kroger's growth through both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions over the decades.

This move aims to capture spending from higher-income customers, who, despite shopping less frequently, spend more per visit, potentially boosting annual sales by $1 billion. The company is also shedding the traditional "dollar store stigma" by offering products up to $5, and more recently, up to $7, and utilizing prime vacant storefronts.

title: Dollar Tree makes an upscale play to fuel sales
summary: Dollar Tree is strategically opening new stores in more affluent areas to attract higher-income customers who spend more per visit. The company aims to boost higher-income shoppers' visit frequency, acknowledging that current inflation trends are also leading more households to seek out discount retailers.

title: The Household Brand Behind Walmart's Great Value Bakery Products

Other brands like Sara Lee Bakery (now Bimbo Bakeries) and Flowers Foods have also supplied Great Value products, highlighting Walmart's strategy of partnering with established manufacturers for its private-label goods.

title: Changes At Dollar Tree: It's Not What We Saw Coming
summary: Dollar Tree has significantly altered its pricing model, moving away from its traditional "dollar" concept. Starting in 2021, prices increased from $1 to $1.25, and by late 2025, specific items were being sold for as much as $10. This shift has attracted more high-income shoppers, leading to questions about where middle-income earners will shop if Dollar Tree continues to resemble other big-box retailers.

summary: PVH Corp. has partnered with OpenAI to integrate custom AI capabilities across its operations, including product design, demand planning, inventory optimization, and customer engagement. This collaboration, part of PVH's multi-year growth strategy (PVH+ Plan), aims to enhance Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger brands and foster innovation. OpenAI's Chief Commercial Officer highlighted the potential for increased creativity and sector-wide transformation through this deployment.

title: Sonoco Products Company Reports Strong Q4 2025 Results with 29.7% Revenue Growth
summary: Sonoco Products Company reported robust Q4 2025 results, with a 29.7% year-over-year revenue increase to $1.8 billion, surpassing market expectations. This growth was primarily fueled by the acquisition of Eviosys' Metal Packaging EMEA business and strong performance in the Consumer Packaging segment. The company also demonstrated significant profitability enhancement, with adjusted operating profit surging by 47.1% and adjusted EBITDA rising by 10.2%, leading to an optimistic future outlook including a projected 20% improvement in adjusted earnings for 2026.

title: JAKKS Pacific Renews Master Global Toy Partnership with Universal Products & Experiences for Nintendo and Illumination’s The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

This renewal follows the success of their toy collection for "The Super Mario Bros. Movie," which was a top performer in 2023 and 2024.

title: Palo Alto, Opendoor, Carvana And More Stocks With Earnings This Week
summary: The fourth-quarter earnings season is concluding, but significant reports are anticipated this week from major companies. Notable earnings include Palo Alto Networks on Tuesday, Carvana on Wednesday, and Walmart and Opendoor Technologies on Thursday, with analysts providing revenue and EPS expectations for each.

title: Pardon the Disruption: The big question facing Kroger’s new CEO
summary: Kroger's new CEO, Greg Foran, is an experienced operations guru tasked with improving the supermarket chain's core business. While he is expected to streamline operations and enhance the store experience, the article questions whether he can address Kroger's underlying identity crisis in a rapidly changing grocery landscape. The challenge lies in distinguishing Kroger from discounters, specialty grocers, and e-commerce giants, and maintaining relevance beyond just offering a large selection of goods, especially as digital shopping grows and traditional supermarket models lose appeal.

title: Earnings live: eToro surges after Q4 profit beat, Medtronic stumbles, DTE Energy pops
summary: The fourth quarter earnings season is nearing its end, with 74% of S&P 500 companies having reported, showing a blended earnings growth of 13.2%. Key companies like eToro, Medtronic, and DTE Energy have released their results, impacting their stock performance, while several major companies like Walmart, eBay, and DoorDash are set to report next. Investors are focusing on continued themes such as AI, trade policies, and consumer economy trends, with a notable shift in how the market reacts to companies missing earnings estimates.

title: Omnicom Named a Leader in Commerce Services by Independent Research Firm

This achievement highlights Omnicom's strengthened commerce capabilities, following its 2024 acquisition of Flywheel Digital and 2025 acquisition of Interpublic, which provided access to Flywheel Commerce Cloud and Acxiom Real ID. The company's AI-driven platform, Omni, integrates these assets to deliver advanced connected commerce experiences for its clients.

title: Unilever changes its brand discovery calculus with Google Cloud AI pact

This partnership will involve migrating Unilever's data and cloud platforms to Google to build an enterprise-wide AI infrastructure, using tools like Vertex AI for brand discovery and measurement. The CPG giant aims to adapt to changing consumer behaviors where AI assistants will increasingly influence brand discovery and shopping.

title: Pan American Silver Set to Announce Q4 Earnings
summary: Pan American Silver (PAAS) is scheduled to announce its Q4 earnings on February 18, with Wall Street anticipating a significant year-over-year increase in EPS to $0.89 and revenue of $1.12 billion. The company achieved a record silver production of 7.3 million ounces in Q4 and expects continued growth, contributing to a positive long-term outlook despite short-term market fluctuations for precious metals. Analysts have raised price targets for PAAS, citing increased gold and copper price forecasts and strong demand drivers.

title: Strategic Adjustments Continue at Kroger, but Structural Disadvantages Limit Durable Share Gains
summary: Kroger, despite being the largest U.S. dedicated supermarket operator and holding 6% of the grocery market, faces significant structural disadvantages. The company lacks enduring intangible assets and the cost advantages of rivals like Walmart and Amazon, limiting its ability to gain lasting market share. Kroger's strategy to use its 2,700+ stores as fresh food and omnichannel hubs is seen as a necessary move to maintain relevance rather than to carve out a distinct competitive edge.

title: JAKKS Pacific Renews Master Global Toy Partnership with Universal Products & Experiences
summary: JAKKS Pacific has renewed its global toy partnership with Universal Products & Experiences, Illumination, and Nintendo to create a new collection of toys inspired by "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie." title: Costco Stock Fell 5% Last Year and Now Facing Lawsuits: Can it Recover in 2026?

summary: HDFC Bank has acquired a 10% stake in CSC Grameen e-Store, a special purpose vehicle of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, to expand financial services in rural and semi-urban areas. This partnership aims to bridge the digital gap between urban and rural India by leveraging the CSC network for various banking and e-commerce services.

title: Traeger pellet grills are changing backyard BBQ — but are they worth it?
summary: Traeger pellet grills offer an easy way to achieve wood-smoked flavor for backyard BBQ, combining smoking, convection oven, and grilling capabilities with app control. While they excel at low-and-slow cooking and provide exceptional convenience, potential buyers should consider the cost, upkeep, and slower startup times. The article provides a detailed analysis of Traeger models, features, pricing, and real-world trade-offs, aiming to help consumers decide if a Traeger grill is the right fit for their needs.

title: Traeger pellet grills are changing backyard BBQ — but are they worth it?
summary: Traeger pellet grills offer wood-smoked flavor with push-button ease, app control, and smart heat, making them popular for backyard BBQ. While they excel at low-and-slow cooking and provide consistent results, they come with a higher price tag, require electricity, and demand regular cleaning. Despite some tech quirks and a milder smoke flavor compared to traditional smokers, they are a strong option for those prioritizing convenience and consistent quality.

title: 16 Walmart stores in Birmingham-area will become COVID-19 vaccination sites
summary: Sixteen Walmart stores in the Birmingham-Metropolitan area will become COVID-19 vaccination sites. This decision follows discussions between Birmingham City Councilor Hunter Williams and Walmart executives, who recognized the need for vaccination access in underserved areas. These new sites will significantly expand vaccine availability in the region, adding to the existing three vaccination locations.

title: BofA Lifts Coca-Cola (KO) Target as Outlook and Execution Remain Strong
summary: BofA analyst Peter Galbo raised his price target for Coca-Cola (KO) to $88 from $85, reiterating a Buy rating, citing confidence in the company's outlook and execution despite upcoming leadership changes and evolving consumer preferences. Coca-Cola aims for 4% to 5% organic revenue growth in 2026 and is adjusting to lower-sugar trends and the impact of weight-loss drugs by focusing on innovation and offering more affordable packaging. New CEO Henrique Braun is expected to accelerate these efforts, though demand in North America and Asia saw declines in the last quarter of 2025.

title: Ecommerce earnings recap: What you missed from Yeti and La-Z-Boy
summary: This article summarizes the recent ecommerce earnings reports from Yeti and La-Z-Boy, highlighting Yeti's net sales increase driven by ecommerce and AI, and La-Z-Boy's overall sales growth despite struggles with its Joybird brand. It also briefly touches upon the earnings of other major retailers like Etsy, Walmart, and Wayfair, indicating trends in online sales and the impact of AI.

title: Home Depot (HD) Q4 Earnings: What To Expect
summary: Home Depot (HD) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Tuesday morning. Analysts expect a 3.9% year-on-year revenue decline, a reversal from the previous year's increase, and have largely reconfirmed their estimates. The stock is down 1.1% over the last month and has an average analyst price target of $398.45.

title: Philadelphia looting: Business owners cleaning up after nights of looting in Port Richmond
summary: Business owners in Philadelphia's Port Richmond section are cleaning up extensive damage after multiple nights of looting that followed the shooting of Walter Wallace Jr. Many businesses, including a Five Below and Cricket Wireless, were ransacked, with some items being donated instead of resold. The looting has deeply impacted employees, particularly mothers, who rely on their jobs to support their families.

title: Once Upon a Farm’s IPO signals growing interest in better-for-you kids’ food
summary: Organic kids' food brand Once Upon a Farm recently went public, raising $198 million at a $724 million valuation, signaling a growing consumer, retailer, and investor appetite for healthier food options for children. Other brands in the "better-for-you" kids' food and beverage space, like Roxberry and Mission MightyMe, are also experiencing significant growth and retail expansion, driven by millennial and Gen-Z parents who are increasingly critical of ingredients and eager for trusted options. The success of these brands suggests a long-term investment trend in this category, with retailers actively seeking differentiated offerings to attract and retain family shoppers.

title: Ubiquiti UniFi Dream Machine review: This is the router you've been looking for
summary: The Ubiquiti UniFi Dream Machine is reviewed as an ideal router for home networking, offering extensive configuration options, excellent Wi-Fi range, and robust hardware. While it uses Wi-Fi 5 instead of Wi-Fi 6, its prosumer features, scalability, and ease of setup for a Ubiquiti ecosystem make it a standout choice for tinkerers and general users alike, despite its premium price point. The article highlights its strong performance and reliability over two weeks of testing.

title: Markets Brief: Nvidia’s Earnings Preview by the Numbers, and What It Means for Investors
summary: This article provides a preview of Nvidia's upcoming earnings report, highlighting key metrics like revenue and gross margins that investors should watch. It also discusses Nvidia's significant impact on market indexes due to its massive rally since 2023 and analyzes broader Q4 earnings trends, including insights into consumer resilience, housing weakness, increased Big Tech capital expenditure, and the "real economy" benefits from AI buildout. The piece concludes by noting the "missing" impact of AI on GDP figures as reported.

title: Edible Garden Reports 2025 Sustainability Performance Through Walmart’s Project Gigaton
summary: Edible Garden AG Incorporated announced its 2025 sustainability results as part of Walmart's Project Gigaton, highlighting significant progress in reducing supply chain emissions. The company achieved measurable impacts in logistics optimization, water reuse, recycling initiatives, and food waste reduction, demonstrating its commitment to environmental efficiency. These efforts align with both Walmart's climate objectives and Edible Garden's "Zero-Waste Inspired®" mission, reinforcing sustainability as a core operational component.

title: Scented Candle Market Outlook 2026-2036: Global Industry to Surpass USD 6,384.3 million by 2036 at 4.5% CAGR
summary: The global scented candle market is projected to reach USD 6,384.3 million by 2036, growing at a CAGR of 4.5% from USD 4,129.8 million in 2026. This growth is driven by increasing consumer preference for natural, wellness-oriented home fragrance products, premiumization, and sustainable innovations in wax blends and packaging. Jar and container candles, along with hypermarkets/supermarkets, are identified as leading segments, with North America holding the largest market share and China showing the fastest growth.

title: 6 Stocks Driving the 2026 US Stock Market Rotation
summary: In 2026, the US stock market is experiencing a significant rotation, with industrial, consumer defensive, and energy stocks outperforming technology names as investors seek "real economy" assets. Key outperformers include Caterpillar, Walmart, and Exxon Mobil, driven by factors like AI infrastructure buildout, cost-conscious consumer spending, and rising oil prices. Despite their strong year-to-date gains, Morningstar analysts do not consider these leading stocks undervalued.

title: Walmart closes stores, flights canceled & travel bans as mega storm paralyzes US
summary: A mega winter storm is paralyzing the U.S. Northeast with up to two feet of snow, leading to widespread disruptions. Walmart has closed dozens of stores, thousands of flights have been canceled, and travel bans are in effect in New York, New Jersey, and Rhode Island. Experts warn the storm could reach "bomb cyclone" status, causing major impacts across the region.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Deep-Dive: Navigating the 2026 Capex Cycle and the Tariff Windfall
summary: Amazon (AMZN) is at a critical juncture in 2026, balancing massive investments in AI infrastructure and the "Amazon Leo" satellite constellation with a significant and unexpected windfall from potential tariff refunds. This deep-dive explores how these tariff refunds could provide crucial liquidity to offset the "Capex shock" of its $200 billion investment plan, assessing the company's valuation, business model evolution, and competitive landscape. The article highlights Amazon's strategic pivot into high-cost technological frontiers and examines the financial, operational, and geopolitical factors shaping its future.

title: The Honest Company Brings Back Scented Sanitizing Wipes with Naturally Derived Fragrances
summary: The Honest Company has announced the return of its popular scented sanitizing wipes in Lavender and Grapefruit, featuring naturally derived fragrances. These wipes, which kill 99.99% of germs, are designed for the whole family and are available in convenient travel and larger pack sizes. The relaunch reflects customer demand and updates to packaging, with products available on Amazon, Target.com, and soon Walmart.com.

title: Plug To Announce 2025 Fourth Quarter and Year-End Results
summary: Plug Power Inc. (NASDAQ: PLUG) announced it will release its 2025 fourth quarter and year-end financial results on March 2, 2026. The company will host a call and webcast at 4:30 PM ET to discuss the results, which can be accessed via a toll-free number or directly from their website. Plug Power is a global leader in comprehensive hydrogen solutions, building an integrated ecosystem for hydrogen production, storage, delivery, and power generation.

title: Plug Power Inc. to Announce 2025 Fourth Quarter and Year-End Results on March 2, 2026
summary: Plug Power Inc. (NASDAQ: PLUG) is scheduled to announce its 2025 fourth quarter and year-end financial results on March 2, 2026, followed by a conference call at 4:30 PM ET. The company, a leader in hydrogen solutions, will provide insights into its financial performance and ongoing efforts to build a comprehensive hydrogen economy through its integrated ecosystem of production, storage, delivery, and power generation. The article also includes details on recent insider trading activity, hedge fund movements, and analyst ratings for PLUG stock.

title: Kohl’s (KSS) Jumps on New Activist Push: Smart Buy or Value Trap?
summary: Kohl's (KSS) stock is seeing renewed interest due to activist pressure, buyout speculation, and a challenging outlook for middle-income shoppers in the US. While the stock appears statistically cheap compared to the S&P 500 and retail peers, its future depends on management's ability to improve traffic and margins before a recession or a private equity buyout. The article discusses the risks and opportunities for Kohl's, including its real estate assets, Sephora partnership, and sensitivity to US consumer spending.

title: Boston Scientific adds Starbucks CFO, Takeda CEO to board of directors
summary: Boston Scientific has appointed Cathy Smith, CFO of Starbucks, and Christophe Weber, President and CEO of Takeda Pharmaceutical, to its board of directors. These appointments are effective February 18, 2026, and follow the retirements of current directors John Sununu and Yoshiaki Fujimori. Both new board members bring extensive leadership experience in finance, corporate strategy, and global pharmaceutical industries to Boston Scientific.

title: ETFs in Focus as Walmart Loses Its Largest Retailer Title to Amazon
summary: Amazon has surpassed Walmart to become the world's largest retailer by annual revenues, driven significantly by its high-margin technology ecosystem like Amazon Web Services and its advertising business. This shift highlights Amazon's dual role as a retail and technology leader. The article suggests that investors seeking exposure to this trend might consider Amazon-heavy ETFs to mitigate single-stock risks while capitalizing on the growth of digital retail, cloud, and AI sectors.

title: Piper Sandler Adjusts Price Target on Best Buy to $71 From $76, Maintains Neutral Rating
summary: Piper Sandler has adjusted its price target for Best Buy (NYSE: BBY) to $71, down from $76, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This change reflects a revised outlook from the analyst firm. The article also provides a snapshot of Best Buy's current trading information and recent analyst actions from other firms.

summary: Edible Garden AG Incorporated (Nasdaq: EDBL, EDBLW) announced its 2025 sustainability results as part of Walmart’s Project Gigaton, showcasing significant progress in reducing environmental impact. The company reported avoiding 412,537 refrigerated truck miles, reducing diesel fuel demand by 30,734 gallons, conserving 694 barrels of crude oil, recycling 1.89 million gallons of water, and reducing 167 metric tons of food waste. These achievements underscore Edible Garden's commitment to its Zero-Waste Inspired® mission and responsible supply chain management.

title: Costco’s minimum $20 rule expected to rise in DAYS across all 640 stores
summary: Costco is set to increase its minimum hourly wage from $20 to $21 in March 2026, affecting starting workers across its 640 US locations. This is part of a three-year labor agreement and aims to maintain industry-leading pay, potentially challenging competitors like Target and Walmart who currently offer lower starting wages. The pay raise will extend to US and Canadian employees, with another increase to $22 per hour scheduled for March 2027.

title: Carter’s, Inc. to Report Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Results on Friday, February 27, 2026
summary: Carter's, Inc. (NYSE: CRI) will release its fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025 financial results on Friday, February 27, 2026, before the market opens. The company will also host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. EST on the same day to discuss the results and provide a business update. title: 5 Oversold Stocks to Buy Before They Rebound

title: XPO will deploy EV trucks in California in April
summary: XPO plans to deploy electric-powered straight trucks in California's San Pedro Bay — including Los Angeles, Long Beach, and San Jose — in April. The LTL carrier will use these smaller, lighter-duty box trucks for city deliveries, particularly in residential areas. While the company faces mileage and charging infrastructure challenges with Class 8 vehicles, this move aligns with California's toughening environmental standards requiring zero-emission vehicles.

title: MATTEL INC /DE/ SEC 10-K Report
summary: Mattel Inc. released its 2025 10-K report, revealing a 1% decrease in net sales to $5,347.6 million and a 27% decrease in net income to $397.6 million compared to 2024, primarily due to increased sales adjustments, cost inflation, and higher expenses. Despite financial declines, the company is implementing strategic initiatives like expanding direct-to-consumer reach, scaling digital play, and leveraging AI, alongside a new $1.50 billion share repurchase program. Mattel faces challenges from evolving consumer preferences, intense competition, global economic factors, customer concentration, and regulatory changes.

title: Kroger has begun to close 60 stores after failed merger
summary: Kroger has started closing 60 stores across the nation, primarily due to underperformance, following the failure of its proposed merger with Albertsons. The company plans to open new high-growth potential stores at an accelerated rate, confirming closures in states like Texas and others, while laying off 1,000 employees. The closures represent about 5% of its 1,239 stores.

These appointments coincide with the retirement of Scott Neal, the current Chief Merchandising Officer.

title: Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Given Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages

Ellison, recently sold 18,000 shares, and Lowe's is set to host its Q4 and fiscal 2025 earnings call on February 25th.

title: Vestmark Advisory Solutions Inc. Acquires 59,004 Shares of Walmart Inc. $WMT
summary: Vestmark Advisory Solutions Inc. significantly increased its stake in Walmart Inc. by 308.6% in Q3, purchasing 59,004 shares to hold a total of 78,124 shares valued at approximately $8.05 million. This institutional accumulation comes despite notable insider selling by Walmart executives, who sold shares worth over $25 million in the last quarter, contributing to some near-term selling pressure. Walmart recently reported strong earnings, beating analyst expectations, and received multiple price target increases from analysts, resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock.

title: Walmart rolls out Scintilla In-Store across stores
summary: Walmart Data Ventures has launched Scintilla In-Store, a new retail execution platform for supplier field representatives. This application integrates tools, real-time store data, performance metrics, and supplier tasks into a single interface, aiming to reduce out-of-stocks and enhance in-store execution. The platform builds on Walmart's existing Scintilla insights platform and is a rebranded version of Volt, acquired by Walmart in 2022.

title: Analysts Estimate Target (TGT) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
summary: Target (TGT) is anticipated to report a year-over-year decline in earnings and lower revenues for the quarter ending January 2026. Analysts expect quarterly earnings of $2.17 per share, a 10% decrease, and revenues of $30.54 billion, down 1.2%. The Zacks Earnings ESP indicates a potential negative surprise, with the most accurate estimate being lower than the consensus.

title: Target Corporation $TGT Shares Acquired by Lansforsakringar Fondforvaltning AB publ
summary: Lansforsakringar Fondforvaltning AB publ increased its stake in Target Corporation by 6.8% in Q3, now owning 150,256 shares worth $13.48 million. Wall Street analysts currently hold a "Hold" consensus rating for TGT, with an average 12-month price target of $105.03.

title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Freshpet stock rating on growth outlook
summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Freshpet (NASDAQ:FRPT) to Overweight from Equalweight, increasing its price target to $90.00. The firm anticipates Freshpet's topline growth to re-accelerate in 2026, driven by easier comparisons and reduced competitive risk from General Mills. Morgan Stanley also sees an underappreciated opportunity for Freshpet through Walmart's and Amazon's expansion in same-day grocery delivery.

title: Greene Concepts Announces Major Be Water Expansion in Walmart Stores Across the Southeast
summary: Greene Concepts Inc. (OTCID:INKW) announced a significant expansion of its Be Water brand within Walmart stores across the Southeastern United States. title: Smucker sues Trader Joe’s, as Uncrustables shifts from freezer to fridge
summary: J.M. Smucker is reformulating its Uncrustables line to be fridge-ready, eliminating the need for thawing, and is expected to reach $1 billion in sales by 2026. Concurrently, the company is intensifying its battle against imitators, having sued Trader Joe's for trademark infringement over its "Crustless Peanut Butter Strawberry Jam Sandwiches." Other competitors like The Dropout Companies and Kraft Heinz have also introduced similar products, prompting Smucker to defend its market position and brand image, particularly through legal action and product innovation.

title: Steven Madden faces earnings test as private label pressures mount
summary: Steven Madden Ltd. is facing an earnings test, with investors keen to see if recent tariff relief and branded footwear strength can counter issues in its wholesale and private label businesses. Analysts anticipate lower-than-street EPS guidance for fiscal 2026 due to private label weakness, slower off-price sales, and increased SG&A, despite a general Buy rating on the stock. The company's performance will hinge on its ability to manage mass merchant pricing resistance and capitalize on fashion trends in its core brands.

title: Is Costco Wholesale Stock Underperforming the S&P 500?
summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) has seen strong recent performance, with its stock gaining 9.7% over the past three months and 14.3% year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500. However, COST underperformed the S&P 500 over the past 52 weeks, dipping 4.7% compared to the index's 13.7% returns. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Costco, expecting an 8% upside from current price levels.

title: Walmart or Dollar General: Which Is the Better Retail Play Right Now?
summary: This article compares Walmart and Dollar General, two retail giants with different business models, to determine which offers a better investment opportunity. While Walmart boasts unmatched scale and stability, Dollar General is showing strong operational recovery and expansion. The author concludes that Dollar General currently appears to be the more attractive bet for investors seeking greater upside potential, reflected in its Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) compared to Walmart's Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

title: Walmart or Dollar General: Which Is the Better Retail Play Right Now?
summary: Walmart and Dollar General are both strong retail players with distinct business models. Walmart offers unmatched scale and stability, while Dollar General is undergoing an operational recovery with improving fundamentals. For investors seeking greater upside potential and a more moderate valuation, Dollar General appears to be the more attractive bet currently.

title: Kroger among best grocery stores in the US, survey reveals. See list
summary: A new study by the American Consumer Satisfaction Index (ACSI) has ranked Trader Joe's as the top grocery store in the United States, with a score of 86. Kroger, headquartered in Cincinnati, placed 12th with a score of 78, the same as last year. The ACSI survey, which polled 31,000 shoppers, evaluates stores based on categories like food quality, checkout speed, and store cleanliness.

title: Home Depot Says Homeowners Are Weary From Economic Pressures
summary: Home Depot reported a decline in sales and net income as homeowners, burdened by economic pressures such as inflation and high financing costs, are postponing large remodeling projects. Executives noted reduced demand for projects associated with home buying and selling, attributing it to low consumer confidence and concerns about job security and housing affordability. This trend is exacerbated by historically low housing turnover rates, as high home prices and interest rates deter both buyers and sellers.

title: Kroger Rolls Out GLP-1 Savings Programs
summary: Kroger has launched GLP-1 weight management savings programs, allowing customers to fill any provider's GLP-1 prescription at Kroger pharmacies with access to manufacturer discounts and insurance guidance. The initiative also integrates nutritional support with virtual dietitian appointments and the OptUP program, addressing the growing demand for GLP-1 drugs and competition in the medication-supported weight management market.

title: Colgate-Palmolive plans to defend DEI criteria for board selection, letter shows
summary: Colgate-Palmolive has informed the National Legal and Policy Center that it will ask investors to vote against a proposal to remove DEI-related criteria for board selection. This decision aligns Colgate with a few other companies like Costco and Apple that are maintaining their DEI policies despite pressure from conservative groups and the Trump administration. The company emphasizes the importance of a broad range of skills, experiences, perspectives, and backgrounds on its board, noting that about two-thirds of its net sales come from outside the U.S.

title: Form 424B2 TORONTO DOMINION BANK
summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering Autocallable Contingent Interest Barrier Notes linked to the performance of common stocks from Citigroup Inc., Oracle Corporation, and Walmart Inc. These notes offer a contingent interest payment of approximately 24.65% per annum if certain conditions are met, and can be automatically called. The document details the payment mechanics, risk factors including potential loss of principal, and tax implications for investors.

title: Jonathan Jackson of IL01 makes notable trades in Citigroup, Coupang, and others
summary: Jonathan Jackson, representing Illinois's 1st congressional district, has engaged in several notable stock transactions. He purchased shares in Citigroup, GE Vernova, ViaSat, and Walmart, with transaction values ranging from $15,001 to $100,000, primarily through a Morgan Stanley Trust Account. Additionally, Jackson sold shares in Coupang, Palantir Technologies, Shopify, and T-Mobile US, also via the same trust account, and made smaller trades in JP Morgan Chase and Robinhood Markets through a Rollover IRA.

title: Smart eyewear pioneer Lucyd expands premium brand portfolio with Reebok, Eddie Bauer, and Nautica Collections
summary: Innovative Eyewear, operating under the Lucyd brand, is expanding its smart eyewear portfolio by partnering with established consumer brands like Reebok, Eddie Bauer, and Nautica. This strategy aims to accelerate adoption by integrating smart eyewear technology into recognized brands, targeting diverse customer segments, and leveraging existing retail channels. The company's Lucyd app, featuring the first voice interface for ChatGPT, serves as a unified platform across all its products, indicating a move towards broad utility and device-agnostic AI accessibility.

title: JD.com, Inc. (JD) Beats Stock Market Upswing: What Investors Need to Know
summary: JD.com, Inc. (JD) recently closed up 1.03% to $27.51, outperforming the S&P 500. title: Citi Raises Costco (COST) Price Target to $1,000 from $990

title: Rothschild Sets $165 Target on Dollar Tree (DLTR) as Rating Moves to Buy
summary: Rothschild & Co Redburn upgraded Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) to Buy from Neutral, setting a $165 price target due to solid execution and accelerating momentum observed in the company's third-quarter 2025 earnings. Dollar Tree reported a 4.2% increase in comparable sales and an adjusted EPS of $1.21, exceeding guidance. The company also raised its full-year outlook, projecting robust sales and EPS growth.

title: Rep. Jonathan L. Jackson Buys Walmart Inc. (NASDAQ:WMT) Stock

Despite these positives, there has been significant insider selling by senior executives, which could impact short-term sentiment.

This marks the company's first venture into the ready-to-drink market, offering three flavors inspired by their popular drive-thru beverages: Blondie, Brunette Brownie, and Banana Bread. Each 11-ounce can will retail for $2.98.

title: Morgan Stanley Lowers Wyndham Hotels & Resorts (NYSE:WH) Price Target to $85.00

title: Ocado turns to store-based e-commerce fulfillment as it looks beyond Kroger
summary: Ocado is pivoting its strategy towards store-based e-commerce fulfillment to secure new partnerships with U.S. grocers, following the conclusion of its exclusive agreement with Kroger. The company is now actively engaging with retailers interested in its smaller, automated store-based system, which CEO Tim Steiner believes is viable for stores generating over $5 million in annual online sales. This shift comes as Ocado faces setbacks, including Kroger scaling back its online fulfillment network and layoffs, pushing the company to adapt to the growing demand for same-day, in-store-centric fulfillment solutions.

title: Marcus & Millichap closes sale and arranges financing for 27,541 s/f retail condo at 156-168 Bleecker St.

title: Duke Energy reaches agreements with NC, SC officials to combine 2 utility companies

title: From industrial site to 3D wetland: Crescent Island's immersive new exhibit

The exhibition aims to educate visitors on ecological conservation, wetland restoration, and biodiversity through immersive visual storytelling and interactive digital scenes.

The launch addresses the often-overlooked aspect of gum care in overall wellness, providing consumers with simple, effective solutions for long-term gum protection.

This strategic hire aims to deepen the firm's presence and offer differentiated alternative investment strategies to a growing European investor base.

The goal is to create a brighter, more inviting shopping experience with expanded aisles, enhanced online grocery pickup areas, upgraded pharmacies, and improved in-store technology.

During these partial closures, pharmacies and fuel stations will remain open, while the main shopping areas will be temporarily closed to minimize disruption. The program will test new remodeling processes in select stores across several states, with an eye towards improving efficiency and customer experience.

title: General Mills bringing beloved La Tiara taco shells back - and going national
summary: General Mills is relaunching the beloved La Tiara taco shells, a Kansas City brand with a cult following, making them available nationally for the first time exclusively at Walmart starting in April. The decision was influenced by strong fan demand, including a Facebook group with over 20,000 members. This relaunch will also bring back over 100 jobs to the Kansas City area with the reopening of the brand’s Gladstone facility.

title: Build-A-Bear launches first-ever wholesale partnership
summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop has initiated its first-ever wholesale partnership with Walmart, making several of its products available in over 1,500 Walmart stores nationwide for a limited time until May 15. This strategic move aims to introduce Build-A-Bear to new customers and coincides with the announcement of CEO Sharon Price John's retirement and Chris Hurt's appointment as the new CEO. The exclusive Walmart offerings include licensed Bluey characters, Mini Beans, and customizable classic Tan Bears.

title: Kroger CEO focused on bananas, among other things
summary: Kroger's new CEO, Greg Foran, is reportedly initiating significant changes, with a surprising focus on the company's banana procurement process. Sources close to the grocer indicate Foran is frustrated by Kroger buying bananas through brokers instead of directly from growers, a practice that impacts a major revenue driver and traffic builder for the supermarket chain. Additionally, Foran is believed to be planning major changes to Kroger's labor force due to perceived overstaffing and underperformance, and re-evaluating the company's extensive partnership with Ocado for automated fulfillment centers.

title: Walmart testing rapid remodel process in some Neighborhood Market stores
summary: Walmart is initiating a new rapid remodel process for select Neighborhood Market stores in six states, aiming to complete renovations in just four weeks. This approach is designed to minimize disruption for customers, with stores temporarily closing but pharmacies and fueling stations remaining open. The remodeled stores will feature expanded aisles, new layouts, digital price signage, and upgraded online grocery pickup and pharmacy areas.

title: Tiptree : PROXY 2025 Proxy Statement

title: Walmart names former U.S. Attorney as chief legal officer

title: Think Walmart Has the Cheapest Groceries? These 6 Stores Are Actually Cheaper

Six specific grocery chains, including Costco, BJ's, Lidl, Aldi, WinCo, and H-E-B, were identified as being cheaper than Walmart, urging consumers to reconsider their shopping habits to save money.

Additionally, the digital labels can assist workers in locating items for online orders, with over half of all U.S. Walmart locations already having made the switch.
